authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(125) — Sabrina Lake (Lake Sabrina, Inyo Co.)
CCR T14 §7.50(a) — General provisions for the §7.50(b) special-regulation waters list
sources.quotedText: Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Wikipedia — Lake Sabrina, Inyo County, California (infobox coordinates, citing GNIS Feature ID 265702; used because the USGS GNIS ArcGIS gazetteer (nationalmap.gov geonames MapServer/7, "Other Hydrographic Features") has no queryable record for gaz_id 265702 or for the names "Lake Sabrina"/"Sabrina Lake" in Inyo County, CA, despite adjacent gaz_ids 265697/265699 resolving fine)
sources.quotedText: {{Coord|37.2072689|N|118.618698|W|region:US-CA_type:waterbody|format=dms|display=inline,title}} — cite gnis|265702|Lake Sabrina — matching the file's recorded coordinate (-118.6187, 37.2073) to within ~5m.

regulations.humanSummary: Open last Saturday in April through November 15
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag; per CCR §7.50(a), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so the possession limit is also 5 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag and possession limit

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
